title: lasiocephalus subinvolucratus
intro: Lasiocephalus subinvolucratus, also known as Wild Potato or Wild Sweet Potato, is a type of tuberous perennial plant native to South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Lasiocephalus subinvolucratus include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and improve digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and processing the roots or leaves, and are often used to treat various ailments, including diabetes and gastrointestinal issues.
